title: Ruth Slenczynska: A Piano Star Who Played for Presidents
summary: Ruth Slenczynska was a very famous pianist. She was born in California in 1925. Her father was a musician and taught her to play the piano when she was very young. She gave her first concert at age four and played with an orchestra at age seven. People said she was one of the greatest child prodigies, like Mozart. She played for five U.S. presidents, including Harry Truman. They played a duet together at the White House. Ruth also studied with the famous composer Rachmaninoff. She was his last living student. She kept playing the piano into her 90s and released her final album in 2022. Ruth died at age 101 in California. She was a teacher and inspired many people. Her friend said heaven gained a very special angel.

background_read: Ruth Slenczynska was a child prodigy, meaning she was very talented at a young age. She started playing piano when she was a toddler and gave concerts as a little girl. Her father was a violinist and teacher who helped her become a great musician. She studied with the famous composer Rachmaninoff, who wrote beautiful piano music. Ruth played for many important people, including presidents. She also taught other people how to play piano. She lived a long life and made music until she was very old.
Article_Structure: WHO: Ruth Slenczynska, a famous pianist.
WHAT: She was a child prodigy who played piano for presidents and studied with Rachmaninoff.
WHERE: She was born in California and lived in Europe and the United States.
WHY: She became a pianist because her father wanted her to be a musician and she had great talent.

perspectives.description: Ruth was a very talented pianist who amazed people with her skill from a young age.
She taught many students and inspired others with her love for music.
